heading: Additional State plan requirements
label: 3058d Additional State plan requirements

children.text: an assurance that the State, in carrying out any subpart of this part for which the State receives funding under this part, will establish programs in accordance with the requirements of the subpart and this subpart;

an assurance that the State will hold public hearings, and use other means, to obtain the views of older individuals, area agencies on aging, recipients of grants under subchapter X, and other interested persons and entities regarding programs carried out under this part;

an assurance that the State, in consultation with area agencies on aging, will identify and prioritize statewide activities aimed at ensuring that older individuals have access to, and assistance in securing and maintaining, benefits and rights;

an assurance that the State will use funds made available under this part for a subpart in addition to, and will not supplant, any funds that are expended under any Federal or State law in existence on the day before , to carry out each of the vulnerable elder rights protection activities described in the subpart;September 30, 1992

an assurance that the State will place no restrictions, other than the requirements referred to in clauses (i) through (iv) of , on the eligibility of entities for designation as local Ombudsman entities under ;section 3058g(a)(5)(C) of this titlesection 3058g(a)(5) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: public education to identify and prevent elder abuse;

children.label: ii
children.text: receipt of reports of elder abuse;

children.label: iii
children.text: active participation of older individuals participating in programs under this chapter through outreach, conferences, and referral of such individuals to other social service agencies or sources of assistance if appropriate and if the individuals to be referred consent; and

children.label: iv
children.text: referral of complaints to law enforcement or public protective service agencies if appropriate;

children.label: B
children.text: the State will not permit involuntary or coerced participation in the program of services described in subparagraph (A) by alleged victims, abusers, or their households; and

children.label: i
children.text: if all parties to such complaint consent in writing to the release of such information;

children.label: ii
children.text: if the release of such information is to a law enforcement agency, public protective service agency, licensing or certification agency, ombudsman program, or protection or advocacy system; or
a description of the manner in which the State agency will carry out this subchapter in accordance with the assurances described in paragraphs (1) through (6).

children.heading: Privilege
children.label: b Privilege
children.text: Neither a State, nor a State agency, may require any provider of legal assistance under this part to reveal any information that is protected by the attorney-client privilege.
